steps.title: Mark the axis and basic oval
steps.description: Draw a light vertical center line, then sketch a slightly rounded triangle or wide oval for the overall head shape. A fox head is wider at the cheekbones and narrows toward the chin — not a circle.

steps.title: Place the eye-line and muzzle line
steps.description: Draw a faint horizontal line across the middle of the head — this is the eye-line. About halfway between that and the chin, draw another horizontal line for the base of the muzzle. These two guides keep the features from floating out of place.

steps.title: Build the wedge-shaped muzzle
steps.description: Between the muzzle line and the chin, sketch a protruding wedge shape — the fox's long snout. It narrows toward the nose and has a slight downward taper. Place the small triangular nose at the very tip.

steps.title: Place the eyes and ears
steps.description: On the eye-line, place two almond-shaped eyes equidistant from the center axis. Then add two large, upright triangular ears at the top of the head — fox ears are tall relative to the head and set wide apart. Leave a gap between the ears at the crown.

steps.title: Refine the outer silhouette
steps.description: Soften the overall head outline into the characteristic fox silhouette: wide at the cheeks, narrowing to a pointed chin. The cheek fur often flares slightly outward — a gentle convex curve on each side.

steps.title: Add inner ear detail and facial markings
steps.description: Sketch a smaller triangle inside each ear for the inner ear. Add a light dividing line down the muzzle and a small curved line for the mouth beneath the nose. A subtle darker patch around each eye helps the alert expression read clearly.

steps.title: Suggest fur with directional strokes
steps.description: Resist the urge to fill the whole face with texture. Instead, use short, outward-pointing strokes only at the edges — along the cheeks, the ear tufts, and the chin — to suggest the thickness of fur. The center of the face stays relatively smooth.
